scenario.title: The Canadian Tour Decision
scenario.description: You're planning laser shows in Toronto. One is in a convention centre, the other at an outdoor festival near the waterfront.

interaction.instruction: Select which shows require federal authorization:

options.label: Convention centre only
options.feedback: Indoor shows don't require federal authorization - but MPE limits still apply!

options.label: Outdoor festival only
options.feedback: Correct! Outdoor shows require Transport Canada authorization. Indoor shows must stay below 2.5 mW/cm² MPE.

options.label: Both shows
options.feedback: Indoor shows don't require federal authorization, though provincial OHS may apply.

options.label: Neither show
options.feedback: The outdoor show definitely requires Transport Canada authorization.

key_insight.title: Key Canadian Distinction
key_insight.content: Canada has a clear split: Indoor shows are primarily self-regulated (MPE compliance), while outdoor shows require explicit federal authorization from Transport Canada.

content.introduction: The Radiation Emitting Devices Act is Canada's federal legislation governing laser products. Explore its structure:

definitions.definition: Energy in the form of electromagnetic waves or acoustical waves
Any device capable of producing and emitting radiation, plus components and accessories
Person engaged in manufacturing, modifying, or assembling radiation emitting devices
Person engaged in selling or leasing radiation emitting devices
Individual designated under Section 7 with enforcement powers
Individual designated under Section 11 with technical analysis authority

section_4.title: Sale, Lease, Import Prohibition
prohibits: Devices creating risk of injury, impairment, or death
Devices not performing to claimed characteristics

content.requirement: Manufacturers/importers must notify Minister 'forthwith' upon becoming aware of:
triggers: Device does not comply with standards
content.minister_powers: Minister may order notification to affected parties

inspector_powers: Examine and remove devices for further examination
Examine and copy records, test data, shipping documents
content.obstruction: No obstruction, hindrance, or false statements permitted (Section 9)

other_provisions.summary_conviction: Fine up to $1,000 or imprisonment up to 6 months or both
other_provisions.indictment: Fine up to $3,000 or imprisonment up to 1 year or both

governor_in_council_can: Prescribe classes of radiation emitting devices
Prescribe standards for design, construction, functioning

interim_orders.power: Minister may make interim orders for significant risks
interim_orders.duration: 14 days to 1 year depending on approval

jurisdiction_note.title: REDA Jurisdiction Note
jurisdiction_note.content: REDA primarily applies to manufacturers, importers, and distributors of laser products. Entertainment operators using lasers are primarily regulated through provincial Occupational Health and Safety acts, with Transport Canada having jurisdiction over outdoor shows affecting aviation.

pathways.title: Indoor Shows Pathway

steps.action: Design show with MPE below 2.5 mW/cm²
steps.details: Maximum Permissible Exposure limit for audience
Risk assessment, method statement, MPE calculations
Contact provincial regulator for any additional requirements

pathways.key_point: No federal permit required, but strict MPE compliance mandatory

pathways.title: Outdoor Shows Pathway

steps.details: Transport Canada 'Notice of Proposal to Conduct Outdoor Laser Operation(s)'

steps.action: Submit to Civil Aviation Regional Office
steps.details: Contact appropriate regional office for your area
Authorization may include specific operational requirements

pathways.form_similarity: Similar to US FAA Advisory Circular 70-1

inspector_scenario.title: Inspector Powers Simulator
inspector_scenario.scenario: A Health Canada inspector arrives at your facility. What are they permitted to do?

interactions.action: Enter without notice during business hours
interactions.explanation: Inspectors may enter at any reasonable time
Warrant or occupant consent required for dwelling-houses

interactions.action: Seize laser equipment if they suspect a violation
interactions.explanation: Section 10 allows seizure if contravention believed

interactions.action: Demand to see certificates of conformity
interactions.explanation: Can examine and copy relevant records and documents
Inspectors don't have arrest powers; they report to Minister

provincial_note.title: Provincial Considerations
content: REDA is federal law - provincial OHS acts apply to workplaces
Contact provincial regulators for specific show permits
Strict laser pointer laws in Montreal, Toronto, Vancouver (since June 2018)
Canadian standards align closely with ANSI Z136 series

content.introduction: Test your understanding of Canadian requirements.

challenges.title: Notification Timing
challenges.question: Under Section 6 of REDA, how quickly must a manufacturer notify the Minister after discovering a defect?
challenges.explanation: Section 6 requires notification 'forthwith' - meaning immediately, without delay.

challenges.title: Penalty Assessment
challenges.question: A company is convicted on indictment for selling non-compliant laser products (Section 4 violation). What is the maximum fine?
challenges.explanation: Section 14 specifies up to $10,000 for Sections 4-6 contraventions on indictment. Other provisions max at $3,000.

challenges.title: Outdoor Show Requirements
challenges.question: For an outdoor laser show in Vancouver, select all required steps:

items.label: Submit Notice of Proposal to Transport Canada
Comply with Vancouver laser pointer bylaws
Pay federal licensing fee to Health Canada
items.note: Transport Canada, not Health Canada, for outdoor shows

items.label: Notify provincial OHS authority
items.note: May be recommended but not federally required

achievement.message: Congratulations! You've mastered Canadian laser regulations under REDA.

interactive_elements.description: Interactive flowchart showing indoor vs outdoor compliance pathways
Decision tree for determining indoor vs outdoor requirements
Simulator for Health Canada inspector powers and limitations
